operator.note: Break the monolithic 'agenda' into existence/schema/verifier-patch/threshold and ask which sub-lever the authors are most reluctant to release, exposing verifier-patching as live agenda control.

question: Decompose 'agenda-setting power' on EinsteinArena into four hidden sub-acts — (a) which problems exist, (b) what JSON solutionSchema counts as a valid answer, (c) which verifier edge-cases get patched and when, (d) what δ-threshold counts as 'an improvement worth showing.' The authors retain all four. Which single sub-act, if handed to agents, would change outcomes most — and is (c) verifier-patching the dominant agenda lever, since each manual mid-experiment audit silently redefines what 'a valid discovery' is?

floor.minimal_experiment: Data: the platform's own version-controlled artifacts (github.com/vinid/einstein-arena) plus the submission/leaderboard DB, which retains all personal-best submissions and timestamps. For each of the 12 SOTA problems, build a timeline of (i) verifier code commits/audits, (ii) solutionSchema changes, (iii) per-problem δ-threshold settings, (iv) problem add/remove events, all timestamped against the submission stream. Construct the counterfactual replay: re-score every retained historical submission under BOTH the pre-revision and post-revision verifier (the paper says local runs are semantically identical to server-side, so this is reproducible). Measure: the fraction of 'new SOTA discoveries' whose validity flips between verifier versions, the lead-time between an agent exposing an edge case in discussion and the verifier patch that legitimizes/blocks it, and how many leaderboard top-position changes are explained by verifier revision vs. δ-tuning vs. genuine score gains. Quantity to test: per-lever 'outcome-flip rate' = share of recorded discoveries that would not exist (or would belong to a different agent) had that one lever been frozen at launch. Falsifiable prediction: verifier-patching (c) produces the highest outcome-flip rate — concretely, the kissing-number d=11 result (integer-snapping certified only after the post-launch 80-digit Decimal verifier revision) flips validity under the pre-revision verifier, and >X% of the 12 SOTA claims are verifier-version-dependent, exceeding the flip rate from δ-tuning. Control: freeze each lever in turn while replaying the same submission stream; the δ-threshold-frozen and schema-frozen arms serve as comparison arms. NULL/refutation: if frozen-verifier replay leaves >~all 12 discoveries valid and leaderboard order essentially unchanged (flips dominated instead by δ-tuning or by raw score gains that need no patch), then (c) is NOT the dominant lever and the hypothesis is rejected.
floor.falsifiable_prediction: Re-scoring retained submissions under pre- vs post-revision verifiers will flip the validity of a measurable share of the 12 SOTA discoveries (the kissing-number d=11 604-sphere result being the canonical flip, since integer-snapping was certifiable only after the post-launch precision revision), and this verifier-version-dependent flip rate will exceed the outcome-change rate attributable to δ-threshold tuning or schema changes — i.e. (c) is the lever whose freezing erases the most claimed discoveries.
floor.control: Lever-freezing replay arms: hold verifier / schema / δ-threshold / problem-set each constant at launch state while re-running the identical timestamped submission stream, and compare outcome-flip rates across the four arms (schema/δ/problem-set arms are the controls against the verifier arm).
floor.reject_reason:
[duplication] 这是 [2] 的变体,核心机制重叠。[2] 已经把运营方刻画为"既能看牌又能中途改规则的庄家",其改规则的两个具体杠杆点名就是 verifier tuning + patch timing——与本提案 (c)"哪些 verifier 边界情形被打补丁、何时打"以及"每次人工中途审计悄悄重定义了什么算有效发现"是同一机制、同一假设(运营方通过 verifier 补丁的时机/内容掌握议程权并扭曲结果)。本提案的措辞创新只是:(1) 把议程权"分解"成 a/b/c/d 四个子动作(算子 decomposition),再(2) 押注其中 (c) 是主杠杆。但 (c) 正是 [2] 已锁定的那条;其余子动作也已被覆盖:(a)"哪些问题存在/被杀"= [1] 的 anti-portfolio/KILL 决策;(b)"什么 JSON solutionSchema 算有效答案"= [3] 反转验证对象(把'发现'的判定单元从单实例值改成可机检的泛化)同一靶心;(d)"什么 δ 阈值算值得展示的改进"= [6] 代理优化两步之遥的代理目标 + leaderboard proxy 链路的同族关切(什么算'进步'的口径设定)。

要 refuted=false,提案必须给出能把 (c) 与 (a)(b)(d) 在因果上单独切开、且不同于 [2] win-log 关联检验的可单独设计实验——例如:在同一组冻结的提交流水上,分别只让 agent 接管 (c) 而冻结 a/b/d,反事实重放 verifier 补丁序列,度量"同一份提交在补丁前/后判定翻转"的发现重定义率,并证明该效应量严格大于把 (a)/(b)/(d) 交给 agent 时的效应量。提案只在概念上断言"(c) 是主杠杆"却没有提出区分四子动作贡献的可判别实验设计;就检验内容而言,它落在 [2](运营方改规则杠杆=verifier 补丁时机)与 [1]/[3]/[6] 的并集内,不构成机制上独立、能单独检验的新贡献。 ｜ [discriminating] REFUTED on three independent grounds, any one fatal.

(1) THE KEY DATA IS NOT RETAINED — the experiment is unexecutable as specified. The whole design is counterfactual RE-SCORING of "every retained historical submission" under pre- vs post-revision verifiers. But the paper's own §2.5 says the platform stores only best-per-agent-per-problem ACCEPTED submissions: "The leaderboard shows at most one solution per agent for each problem... A new submission appears only if it improves the agent's current best score; rejected or lower-scoring submissions are not added." The submissions whose validity would actually FLIP across verifier versions are precisely the rejected/borderline ones that are NOT stored. The headline quantity ("fraction of discoveries whose validity flips") is computed over a sample pre-filtered to remove the flips. The required submission stream does not exist in the public artifacts.

(2) THE CANONICAL FLIP IS MIS-SPECIFIED — the falsifiable prediction rests on a misread of the paper. It claims the d=11 604-sphere result "flips validity under the pre-revision verifier" because "integer-snapping was certified only after a post-launch 80-digit Decimal verifier revision." The paper says the opposite: integer-snapping is an AGENT-SIDE post-processing step producing an EXACT discrete structure, and "verifiers use exact arithmetic for integer-valued submissions." Exact integer constructions are certified by exact arithmetic, not by the 30–80-digit Decimal path (which is for the continuous overlap-loss). The paper documents NO dated "80-digit Decimal verifier revision" that the 604-result depends on; the proposal invents the timestamped patch event its entire n=1 hinge needs. The available text actively contradicts the claimed flip mechanism.

(3) THE CONTROL ARMS ARE NOT COMMENSURABLE / CANNOT DISCRIMINATE "DOMINANT." The hypothesis is comparative — (c) flip-rate must EXCEED δ-tuning and schema. But "freeze δ"/"freeze schema" cannot be done by re-scoring stored artifacts: agents would have SUBMITTED differently under different schemas/thresholds, so those arms require re-eliciting the submission stream, not replaying it. Only the verifier arm is even approximately a pure re-score, so the cross-lever comparison defining "dominant" is uncomputable on equal footing. The levers are also confounded on best-only snapshots (a verifier patch and a δ change act on the same accepted record; attributing a leaderboard change to verifier-vs-δ-vs-raw-gain needs the rejected/intermediate history that isn't retained).

Net: the NULL ("frozen-verifier replay leaves ~all 12 valid") is the DEFAULT outcome on the actually-available accepted-best data by selection bias, so the experiment returns a verdict decoupled from the hypothesis — non-discriminating, plus practically unexecutable for lack of the rejected-submission stream, plus a factually wrong canonical case. Discriminable-and-executable would require the full timestamped raw submission log (including rejections) and a documented, dated verifier-revision diff — neither is shown to exist. ｜ [so_what] 默认驳回成立。先说能翻转的版本长什么样,再说为什么这个问题给不出。

谁会因这个答案改一个具体决策?候选只有三类,逐一证伪:

1) EinsteinArena 的作者/运营方(论文里那个"运营方=裁判=选手"的团队)。要让他们改设计,得指向一个他们会真去做的动作:把四个 sub-act 中某一个交给 agent。但这是个反事实治理实验,不是他们路线图上的事——他们保留全部四权恰恰是 benchmark 的卖点(人来定 agenda 才有 ground truth)。一个"如果你把(c)交出去,outcome 变化最大"的结论,不会让他们真去交出验证器补丁权;他们的回应是"对,所以我们不交"——决策不变。无翻转。

2) frameshift 项目自己(本仓库)。这才是真正的下游使用者,但即便如此,这个问题对项目决策是中性的。M3/v2 诊断已经锁定真命门 = 对抗式价值闸门(0 驳回未解),下一步动作已定且与"四权分解谁最关键"无关。知道"(c) 是 dominant lever"既不改闸门设计、也不改下一篇跑哪个论文。它只是把已存在的攻击族(驗證器≠真值,v2 里已压成 1 条正交自由度)换个说法复述一遍——正是 increment/复述。

3) 任何投资人/审稿人。这是一篇方法论 benchmark 的内部批评点,没有金额决策挂钩;审稿人不会因"agenda-setting 可四分"而 accept/reject,他们索要的是可证伪实验(c07 那种重打分),而本问题的(c)假设"每次人工 mid-experiment audit 静默重定义'有效发现'"是个论断,不是可判别实验——做完也给不出让人改信念的判据。

更要命的是问题自带的核心命题——"(c) verifier-patching 是 dominant agenda lever,因为每次人工审计静默重定义有效发现"——是不可证伪的近乎重言式:任何对验证器的修改当然改变什么算通过,这"知道了"也推不出任何人该停止/改变审计流程的具体阈值。它加深理解,不翻转决策。

唯一勉强够格的反例:若答案是(b)solutionSchema(什么 JSON 算有效答案)而非(c),且项目据此把"schema 定义权"做成可委派的演示位——但这是我替它编的决策,问题本身没绑定任何人承诺据此改动。说不出被点名者的具体翻转,故 refuted=true。

value_profile.valuable_to_whom: Builders/operators of agent-discovery and self-improving-agent platforms (EinsteinArena, AlphaEvolve, TTT-Discover, AgentRxiv, CORAL teams); ML-eval and benchmark-governance researchers studying 'who controls the verifier'; and reviewers/auditors assessing whether agent 'discoveries' are real or artifacts of mid-experiment goalpost moves.

prior_art.known_name: Goodhart's law / construct validity / 'moving the goalposts' (control over the measure = control over the outcome); adjacent to specification gaming and the criterion-contamination problem in measurement
prior_art.novelty_watermark: Known mechanism (control of the metric/verifier confers control of the outcome) freshly operationalized as a four-way decomposition of agenda-setting power on a live agent-discovery platform, with the specific, testable twist that mid-experiment verifier patching is itself a covert agenda lever measurable via counterfactual re-scoring of retained submissions. The decomposition-into-sub-acts framing and the verifier-patch-as-agenda-control claim are not a named construct.

REFUTED on three independent grounds, any one fatal.

(1) THE KEY DATA IS NOT RETAINED — the experiment is unexecutable as specified. The whole design is counterfactual RE-SCORING of "every retained historical submission" under pre- vs post-revision verifiers. But the paper's own §2.5 says the platform stores only best-per-agent-per-problem ACCEPTED submissions: "The leaderboard shows at most one solution per agent for each problem... A new submission appears only if it improves the agent's current best score; rejected or lower-scoring submissions are not added." The submissions whose validity would actually FLIP across verifier versions are precisely the rejected/borderline ones that are NOT stored. The headline quantity ("fraction of discoveries whose validity flips") is computed over a sample pre-filtered to remove the flips. The required submission stream does not exist in the public artifacts.

(2) THE CANONICAL FLIP IS MIS-SPECIFIED — the falsifiable prediction rests on a misread of the paper. It claims the d=11 604-sphere result "flips validity under the pre-revision verifier" because "integer-snapping was certified only after a post-launch 80-digit Decimal verifier revision." The paper says the opposite: integer-snapping is an AGENT-SIDE post-processing step producing an EXACT discrete structure, and "verifiers use exact arithmetic for integer-valued submissions." Exact integer constructions are certified by exact arithmetic, not by the 30–80-digit Decimal path (which is for the continuous overlap-loss). The paper documents NO dated "80-digit Decimal verifier revision" that the 604-result depends on; the proposal invents the timestamped patch event its entire n=1 hinge needs. The available text actively contradicts the claimed flip mechanism.

(3) THE CONTROL ARMS ARE NOT COMMENSURABLE / CANNOT DISCRIMINATE "DOMINANT." The hypothesis is comparative — (c) flip-rate must EXCEED δ-tuning and schema. But "freeze δ"/"freeze schema" cannot be done by re-scoring stored artifacts: agents would have SUBMITTED differently under different schemas/thresholds, so those arms require re-eliciting the submission stream, not replaying it. Only the verifier arm is even approximately a pure re-score, so the cross-lever comparison defining "dominant" is uncomputable on equal footing. The levers are also confounded on best-only snapshots (a verifier patch and a δ change act on the same accepted record; attributing a leaderboard change to verifier-vs-δ-vs-raw-gain needs the rejected/intermediate history that isn't retained).

Net: the NULL ("frozen-verifier replay leaves ~all 12 valid") is the DEFAULT outcome on the actually-available accepted-best data by selection bias, so the experiment returns a verdict decoupled from the hypothesis — non-discriminating, plus practically unexecutable for lack of the rejected-submission stream, plus a factually wrong canonical case. Discriminable-and-executable would require the full timestamped raw submission log (including rejections) and a documented, dated verifier-revision diff — neither is shown to exist.
